response.title: Curriculum Development Specialist
response.description: The Curriculum Development Specialist will be responsible for designing, implementing, and evaluating educational programs tailored to meet the diverse needs of students. This role requires collaboration with educators and administrative staff to ensure curriculum alignment with educational standards and best practices. The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of instructional design and have the ability to integrate technology into the curriculum.

required_skills: Experience in curriculum design and development
Proficiency in instructional design models (e.g., ADDIE, SAM)
Familiarity with educational standards and assessment frameworks
Strong project management skills with a track record of meeting deadlines
Ability to analyze and interpret educational data to inform curriculum decisions
Proficient in using educational technology tools (e.g., learning management systems, e-learning software)
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Experience in teacher training and professional development
preferred_skills: Knowledge of Universal Design for Learning (UDL) principles
Experience with grant writing for educational programs
Ability to conduct workshops and training sessions
Understanding of diverse learning needs and inclusion strategies
requirements.education: Bachelor's degree in Education, Curriculum Development, or a related field; Master's degree preferred
